title: Conophallus punctulatus
taxonomy: Conophallus punctulatus is classified in the family of Conophyllaceae, a small family of flowering plants. This family is part of the order Santalales. The Santalales order is a group of parasitic flowering plants that obtain their nutrients by connecting to the roots of host plants. Conophallus punctulatus, as part of the Santalales order, exhibits characteristics of parasitic behavior and unique growth patterns. The classification of Conophallus punctulatus reflects its distinct evolutionary adaptations to survive in its environment.
